text: People around me depend heavily on me.
I actively look for my own flaws and shortcomings and work hard to correct them.
I am an extrovert and feel full of passion and drive for the life I long for.
I feel that most people do not know how to appreciate the inner beauty of life.
I am good at hiding my feelings and prefer receiving to giving.
I am often indecisive and don’t know which direction to go; my biggest enemy is my own fear.
Whether at work or in life, I like to make things fun and relaxed.
I am easily satisfied and rarely bothered; others say I am carefree and mild-tempered.
When my interests are violated or I am treated unfairly, I protest to the end and refuse to swallow my anger.
If things do not go according to my plans, I feel very uncomfortable, irritable, and uneasy.
I enjoy being the center of attention and like the feeling of being needed.
At work, I am meticulous and highly professional.
In clubs or work teams, I dislike group discussions and summary speeches; I need a lot of personal time and space or I feel very uncomfortable.
I often immerse myself in memories of the past.
I value loyalty and dislike being deceived or betrayed.
I often make plans but delay in carrying them out, and the plans frequently do not get implemented because of various things.
I can easily see through others’ weaknesses and remember them; when we are in conflict or my interests are harmed, I attack those weaknesses.
When I see others feeling distressed or overworked, I think it is unnecessary and not worth it.
I am often dissatisfied with the status quo.
My eloquence and persuasiveness make people think I would be a good salesperson.
People who interact with me usually say I am easy to get along with.
I am often attracted by strange or unusual things.
When I feel embarrassed, or when others ask what I am thinking, my mind often goes blank.
I find it hard to oppose my superiors’ opinions; even if their ideas are unreasonable, I still follow them.
In general, I do not easily doubt people’s motives.
My work or school life often requires me to face many different types of people, but I am not afraid to interact with them.
I really enjoy leisure time when I do not have to worry about work and can just relax freely.
I am always anxious about all kinds of things.
I am warm-hearted and often go all out to help others.
At work, I plan every step in advance and organize team tasks so that things go smoothly.
I feel others often lack depth in how they view things, so they find it hard to understand my feelings.
I hate feeling stupid and also hate others thinking I am stupid.
I believe I know better than most people how to enjoy life.
Before making any decision, I collect information from various sources to be fully prepared.
In meetings or discussions, I often play the role of the contrarian and resist many things.
I am easily persuaded and everything is negotiable for me.
Wasting time is something I find terrifying.
I like to rescue others from difficult or embarrassing situations.
People around me see me as capable, competent, and efficient.
I have a pessimistic attitude toward life and feel that many things are predetermined and cannot be changed.
At parties or team activities, I usually stay on the sidelines, watching rather than participating.
I am cautious and need to consider many factors before making decisions.
I can always find the beautiful side of things in life; there is almost nothing I cannot appreciate.
I long for power and like to exercise it.
Despite individual differences, I still think most people are kind and good.
After finishing a task, I often regret not having done it more perfectly.
I care more about others’ feelings than my own.
I strongly desire success.
When things do not go my way, I quickly withdraw.
I like to keep everything neat and organized and keep my home well-arranged.
I often doubt whether I have enough courage and ability to do what I should do.
In my life, there are few things that make me feel truly distressed or unhappy.
I can stay in control of everything I do.
I usually do not get too deeply involved in anything.
I often ruin things because I overlook details.
I feel it is my duty to help others; even if I am unwilling, I push myself to do it.
I like to use progress charts or ranking tables to show my work progress.
I dislike seeing myself as ordinary and believe I am unique in many ways.
People think I am quiet and often cannot tell what I am thinking.
I believe there must be strict rules to tell people what they should and should not do.
I have many beautiful memories of my childhood.
I have not experienced many setbacks or embarrassing situations.
I do not feel that anything is so important that it must be done immediately; it is fine to leave some things until tomorrow.
Friends often come to me to talk things over or seek comfort.
I lack the ability to entertain myself and find it hard to relax.
I use task lists, charts, or similar tools to track my progress.
I often feel lost, sad, or confused.
I am not good at interacting with people and find it very difficult to express my needs.
I care a lot about what others think of my principles and views, whether they approve or oppose.
In my life, everything seems to appear in its best form.
I am an active (even aggressive) and self-affirming person.
I need external pressure or motivation to keep working and taking action.
I often criticize myself and others.
Other people’s dependence on me feels like a burden.
My high efficiency often arouses others’ jealousy.
People around me cannot see how much worry and pressure I carry inside; they only see my outer side.
When facing problems, I first think and study on my own, then discuss with others.
I feel that before doing anything, I should get approval from superiors or elders.
I am almost excessively optimistic and feel that ideals are more important than anything else.
Before starting a task, I look for the most effortless approach because I hate wasting energy.
I strongly dislike others interfering with my decisions or imposing their ideas on me.
I feel a lot of pressure to be loyal and responsible.
I feel that I do not have many personal needs.
I consciously shape a successful and positive image of myself in front of others.
Sometimes I feel slow to respond to ordinary feelings.
I am interested in party games but do not know how to join in and feel like an outsider.
I often become suspicious and am very sensitive to partners’ infidelity or others’ lies.
I wish others could take a lighter attitude toward problems like I do.
I find it hard to show my gentle side and am not good at caring for others.
When there is a dispute, I try to judge both sides fairly and rationally without emotional bias.
Whether at work or in study, I do things very rigorously.
When I carefully prepare a party or choose a gift for others, I often doubt whether it suits them.
To reach my goals, I do not mind adjusting my principles and standards appropriately.
I am easily influenced by the emotions of people around me.
I prefer to work alone rather than in a team.
For me, work is only about duty and responsibility.
When thinking about things, I prefer to focus on their bright side rather than the dark side.
When doing things, I usually choose the path with the least resistance that I can complete easily.
No matter what I do, I take a positive and sunny attitude.
I always feel short of time and have many tasks piled up in front of me.
Sometimes I feel like a victim and that people are taking advantage of me.
In front of others, I present myself as young and lively.
I am very sentimental; even with colleagues I rarely talk to, I feel emotional at parting.
I tend to observe things and keep my thoughts to myself instead of speaking out.
When I have doubts, I must get to the bottom of them and cannot accept vague answers.
I am childlike at heart and can easily share joy with others.
Justice and injustice are topics I care about most.
Sometimes people think I am too lazy.
I like to get to know people with power and status.
I believe a person must be responsible for every moment of their life.
Whenever there is something new or trendy, I am usually among the first to try it.
I believe I have refined taste and feel proud of it.
I am stingy with my time, money, and possessions.
Instead of avoiding what I fear, I try hard to face and overcome it.
I like to record the places I have been and plan future trips.
I consider myself a down-to-earth person.
I see myself as calm and steady and not easily provoked, which makes me proud.
Every night before sleep, I mentally review my day and carefully reflect on my shortcomings.
I feel like I am always under pressure.
I have a strong competitive spirit and crave others’ praise.
I often feel like the one being abandoned; even in a crowd I can feel surrounded by loneliness.
I care more about my own feelings and status than about others.
I keep clear boundaries with even the closest people and hate being forced to do anything.
People think I should come out of my own world and interact with others more.
I feel that I am free and not easily compromising.
I like to do things low-key; even when I achieve something, I prefer to stay in the background and live a steady, modest life.
When I see injustice, I stand up and fight against immoral people and thus easily gain recognition from those who value justice.
I believe loving and being loved are essential parts of life.
When I am working hard on something, I hate others saying it is useless or impossible.
I understand the sadness behind a clown’s smile because I am like that too, hiding pain behind a smiling mask.
My voice is relatively soft, yet people always ask me to speak louder, which makes me feel helpless.
I feel safe when I get close to powerful people, but at the same time I fear their power.
I believe people should not stay in pain for too long; no matter how sad, we should move forward.
I am so hardworking that people around me are amazed at my energy.
I can hardly remember the last time I slept restlessly.
I see myself as a very responsible person.
I think it is necessary to give myself for others when needed.
I would rather participate in planning projects than take on supervisory roles.
My emotions often swing between excitement and depression and are very unstable.
I prefer understanding the structure or outline of things more than the detailed content.
I hate people who escape reality and break the law; I feel they must be brought to justice.
I am full of expectations for the future and often imagine what my life will be like.
I feel many things are irrelevant to my life and not worth letting them affect my mood.
I am more efficient when I follow my own plan than when I follow others’ arrangements.
I care deeply about emotions and relationships.
I can be so absorbed in work that I sometimes forget who I am.
I always feel in a hurry, as if someone is pushing me to keep improving myself.
People say my behavior is too dramatic, but for me it is just a way of expression they do not understand.
I cannot bear to throw things away and tend to collect anything that might be useful.
I do not like being the center of attention.
I am the "happy-go-lucky" one among my friends.
I value the present moment and often feel an urgency to act immediately.
I find it hard to calm down and focus on one thing or to be a quiet listener.
I believe that to gain others’ love and approval, I must first do my very best.
I do not know what to do for myself in leisure time and often let holidays pass in a daze.
I care a lot about appearance.
Art and artistic expression are important to me and help me release emotions.
I often imagine myself as a hero in some field or belonging to an important social class.
When entering or leaving the dorm or office, I try to be very quiet so as not to attract attention.
I do not want to get involved in serious issues.
I plan everything carefully and see things through instead of giving up halfway.
My energy is low; after finishing one thing, I feel I have no strength left for other tasks.
I am often disappointed that my life has not turned out as I imagined.
I feel that what I have done is enough to make me important in people’s hearts.
I vaguely remember that as a child I often worried my father or mother would abandon me.
I think I must achieve a lot before I can attract others’ attention.
Before achieving major success, I tend to ignore everything else.
When evaluating someone, I first judge whether they pose a threat to me.
I prefer to skim the surface of events rather than delve into them deeply.
I do not feel that my existence is very important.
I am very sensitive to others’ malice and become extremely angry when I realize I have been deceived.
I believe my opinions are more important than whether things are good or bad, right or wrong.
I believe work that helps others is more honorable than anything else.
I am a person who forges ahead and strives to make progress.
I try to solve difficult problems by thinking them through.
Sometimes I feel like an exiled noble: life is painful but my spirit remains noble.
I think work is only for survival and cannot understand those who enjoy their work, unless it is very entertaining work.
I like work that is well-organized and systematic.
When others make me uncomfortable, I express my dissatisfaction directly.
I do not let things bother me for long.
